exemplar.title: How does Nike's Dream Crazy campaign utilize diverse athlete narratives and multimodal techniques to challenge societal limitations and promote brand identity?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Nike's Dream Crazy campaign utilize diverse athlete narratives and multimodal techniques to challenge societal limitations and promote brand identity?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Provides solid context on Nikeâs branding evolution and athlete backgrounds
Consistently embeds references and describes visuals with relevant detail
weaknesses: Interpretations are asserted rather than probed in depth
Minor factual slips (inconsistent use of Leo Bakerâs name and pronouns)
Limited depth in exploring slogan implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates satisfactory knowledge and understanding of the Dream Crazy campaign (Band 3). References and visual descriptions generally support the argument, but interpretation remains uneven and occasionally superficial, and factual inaccuracies prevent a higher band.

strengths: Analysis of composition (e.g. Serena Williamâs gaze) shows generally appropriate analytical intent
weaknesses: Analysis often remains descriptive or formulaic
Occasional mislabelling ("empathic" vs. "emphatic") indicates analytical immaturity
Limited exploration of how features shape audience reception
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay moves beyond description with generally appropriate analysis of textual and visual features (Band 3). Observations are relevant but lack the consistent insight and depth required for higher bands.

strengths: Clear, logical structure with introduction, focused body paragraphs, and conclusion
Topic sentences and transitions guide the line of inquiry effectively
Supporting examples are mostly well integrated into discussion
weaknesses: Conclusion reiterates examples rather than fully synthesizing insights
Scope could be narrowed for greater depth
Some citations could be more smoothly embedded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with a clear development of ideas and integration of examples (Band 4). Minor lapses in synthesis and focus keep it from the highest band.

strengths: Employs a formal register with clear academic vocabulary
Overall readability remains high despite minor errors
weaknesses: Frequent grammar and wordâchoice errors (agreement, spelling, pronoun inconsistency)
Punctuation and formatting artifacts disrupt flow
Inconsistent tense and hyphenation undermine precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Language is generally clear with adequate accuracy despite lapses (Band 3). While register and style are appropriate, grammatical and mechanical errors prevent a higher score.

strandSelections.reasoning: Supporting examples (image descriptions, slogans, brief biographical facts) are integrated into sentences rather than appended. Phrases such as âThe low-angle footage â¦ demonstrates the confidenceâ weave evidence with commentary. There are moments where citations could be smoother, but overall the examples are embedded well enough to earn a high-mid band rating.

The student demonstrates a satisfactory grasp of the Dream Crazy campaign, outlining Nikeâs overarching brand narrative and providing contextual information about each athleteâs background. Key social issuesâgender identity, racism and disabilityâare recognised and linked to Nikeâs aspirational ethos, showing more than surface-level familiarity. However, interpretation is uneven; comments on âsocial consciousnessâ or âdream accessibilityâ are asserted rather than probed in depth, and there are minor factual slips (e.g. inconsistent use of Leo Bakerâs name and pronouns). Overall, the knowledge is solid but not yet sustained or persuasive enough for a higher band.

The essay follows a clear structureâintroduction outlining scope, three body sections each focused on a specific advertisement, and a summative conclusionâcreating a mostly cohesive line of inquiry. Topic sentences guide the reader, and transitions between examples are logical. While development could be deeper, the organisational pattern is reliable and shows planning, satisfying the âwell organized and mostly cohesiveâ criteria.

References to the three print advertisements are consistently embedded, and brief external sources (Office Magazine, Reuters) are cited to bolster points about each athleteâs struggles. The visuals are described with some specificity (angle, colour, slogan placement), so the evidence generally underpins the argument. Nonetheless, quotation or close reading of on-screen text remains limited and occasionally generic (âblack-and-white communicates timelessnessâ), preventing the support from being fully effective or âwell-chosen,â hence a mid-band placement.

The register remains formal and appropriate for an HL essay, employing analytical diction and third-person perspective. Occasional colloquial touches (âlast but not leastâ) do not significantly undermine tone. Overall style is consistent with academic expectations, justifying a band where register is âconsistently appropriate to the task.â

Language is largely clear and readable, with correct academic vocabulary (âjuxtaposition,â âcomposition,â âsocietal skepticismâ). Errors in agreement, punctuation and word choice (e.g., âtheir career mainly due to their gender expression,â âself.beliefâ) occur but rarely impede comprehension. This aligns with the descriptor of âclear with adequate accuracy â¦ despite some lapses.â

The essay moves beyond pure description by commenting on composition, leading lines, juxtaposition and pathos, showing generally appropriate analytical intent. For example, the discussion of the Serena Williams close-up links visual tension to themes of prejudice, and the Griffin slogan is read as a device of contrast. Still, many observations remain surface-level or formulaic, with limited exploration of why these choices shape audience reception. Occasional mislabelling ("empathic" for "emphatic") reveals analytical immaturity. This fits the descriptor of âgenerally appropriate analysisâ rather than insightful or convincing.

annotations.comment: Pronoun inconsistency: the text states âthey started skateboardingâ then âhis foster brothers,â which undermines clarity.
The skateboarderâs surname is misspelled as âBarkerâ rather than âBaker,â which could signal lack of attention to detail.
The acronym âLGBTOâ is unconventional; consider using âLGBTQ+â to reflect current inclusive terminology.
The close-up analysis of Serena Williamsâ gaze and composition demonstrates generally appropriate analytical intent.
Rather than noting periods, examine how punctuation and typography affect reading pace and emphasis.
How does the high-contrast editing contribute to themes of defiance and strength in this image?
âAchieved to win 4 olympic gold medalsâ is grammatically awkward; revise to âwon four Olympic gold medals.â
Tense inconsistency appears in âSerena has lived throughâ; past simple (âlivedâ) would be clearer.
The identification of Griffin as the focal point shows attentive analysis of composition.
âEmpathicâ is incorrect in this context; the term âemphaticâ better describes the repeated âMe.â
Use a hyphen in âself-beliefâ rather than a period to maintain consistency with standard usage.
The conclusion reiterates examples rather than synthesizing how these analyses collectively illuminate Nikeâs brand strategy.
The bibliography entry spells âTrancendingâ incorrectly; it should read âTranscending.â
The essayâs research question is articulated clearly and reflects a focused inquiry into narrative and multimodal strategies.
Consider narrowing the scope by specifying which societal limitation or target demographic will be the primary focus to make analysis more manageable.
The introduction provides solid context on Nikeâs branding evolution and sets up the analysis of multiple advertisements effectively.
Pronoun usage for Baker fluctuates between singular âtheirâ and plural references, which can confuse reader interpretation of identity.
The campaign slogan is truncated; provide the full quotation (âYou donât have to change who you are to change your worldâ) for accuracy.
Analysis of the black-and-white palette remains descriptive; discuss how tonal contrast influences emotional resonance.
Explore how the interplay between text and the blurred background intensifies the emotional appeal.
Revise the conclusion to draw connections between visual strategies and the broader societal impact Nike achieves.
Citation style varies between entries; adopt a consistent format (APA, MLA, etc.) to enhance professionalism.
Which referencing style did you follow, and how might you adjust entries to align fully with it?
Remove the OCR artifact â-ieers-2021-11-18/â which disrupts the referenceâs readability.
Overall, the student employs a formal register and clear academic vocabulary consistently throughout the essay.
